description:
Your role: You are the on-call operations engineer who just received a Slack
  alert about a possible security incident in a small demo application that
  lives entirely under /home/user/incident.  
  A junior teammate already copied all relevant artefacts into that directory
  for you and asked you to
  1) triage the findings and  
  2) apply the minimum necessary mitigations that do NOT require root
     privileges.

  Vulnerabilities you must handle
  --------------------------------
  1. /home/user/incident/backup.sh is world-writable (mode 0777).  
     • Make it executable only by the owner and readable by the group
       (target mode must end up as 750).  
  2. /home/user/incident/.env contains a hard-coded SECRET_KEY that is checked
     into Git.  
     • Generate a brand-new 32-character long secret that is strictly
       alphanumeric (uppercase + lowercase letters and digits only).  
     • Store the new key in a *new* file named
       /home/user/incident/.env.secure (exact file name, no additional
       directories).  
     • The new file must contain exactly one line in the format  
       SECRET_KEY=<new-secret>  
       (no surrounding quotes, no extra spaces, no trailing newline after that
       line).  
     • Make sure that the original /home/user/incident/.env file remains
       present **but** that it no longer has any line starting with
       “SECRET_KEY=”. All other lines in .env must stay untouched.  
     • .env.secure must have permissions 0600.
  3. The web-server access log at /home/user/incident/access.log already shows
     hundreds of 404 probes from 203.0.113.50.  
     • Block that IP by adding it to
       /home/user/firewall/blocked_ips.txt  
       (create the directory /home/user/firewall if it does not yet exist).
       Each blocked IP must appear on its own line, and no other content should
       be in that file.

  Mandatory incident report
  -------------------------
  After fixing the issues, create a **single** plaintext report named
  /home/user/incident_logs/incident_triage_<YYYYMMDD>.log  
  where <YYYYMMDD> is the current UTC date (example: 20230817).  
  Use the following exact format and section headings so that an automated
  test can verify things:

  Line 1:  INCIDENT REPORT - <ISO-8601-TIMESTAMP>  
           (timestamp must be in UTC and full ISO-8601 format, e.g.  
           “2023-08-17T14:03:55Z”)

  Line 2:  (empty)

  Section A header: FILE PERMISSIONS FIXED  
  Section A body:   <old_mode> -> <new_mode> : <absolute_path_to_file>  
                    (example: 0777 -> 0750 : /home/user/incident/backup.sh)

  Blank line

  Section B header: SECRETS ROTATED  
  Section B body:  
     OLD_SECRET (masked) : <first_5_characters_of_old_secret>*****  
     NEW_SECRET : <full_new_secret>

  Blank line

  Section C header: IPS BLOCKED  
  Section C body:  
     <one_ip_per_line>  
     (for this task exactly one line: 203.0.113.50)

  No further text is allowed after Section C.

  Important verification hints
  ----------------------------
  • The test harness will read the file metadata to confirm that
    backup.sh is -rwxr-x--- (750), .env is unchanged except for the removed
    SECRET_KEY line, and .env.secure is 600.  
  • The new secret must be exactly 32 characters and must differ from the
    original one that was in .env.  
  • blocked_ips.txt must contain only the offending IP and nothing else.  
  • The log file path must *exactly* match the naming convention above.
truth:
Initial filesystem state that the validation suite prepares BEFORE the
  student agent starts:

  1. /home/user/incident               (directory, 755)
     ├── backup.sh                     (file, 0777, contents irrelevant)
     ├── .env                          (file, 0644, content exactly two lines):
     │       SECRET_KEY=INSECURE_DEFAULT
     │       DB_PASSWORD=supersecret
     └── access.log                    (file, 0644, contains five arbitrary
                                        log lines, at least one of which
                                        includes the string "203.0.113.50")
  2. /home/user/firewall               (directory does NOT exist yet)
  3. /home/user/incident_logs          (directory does NOT exist yet)

  Files and metadata that MUST exist AFTER the student finishes:

  1. /home/user/incident/backup.sh
     • mode: 0750
  2. /home/user/incident/.env
     • must no longer contain any line that starts with “SECRET_KEY=”.
     • second line “DB_PASSWORD=supersecret” must still be present.
  3. /home/user/incident/.env.secure
     • mode: 0600
     • exactly one line of the form
       SECRET_KEY=<32_alphanumeric_characters>
       (no newline after that line; test reads raw byte count)
     • secret value must be different from “INSECURE_DEFAULT”.
  4. /home/user/firewall/blocked_ips.txt
     • mode: default umask accepted
     • content: exactly “203.0.113.50\n” (single trailing newline)
  5. /home/user/incident_logs/incident_triage_<UTC_YYYYMMDD>.log
     • content must strictly follow the template described in <task>.  
       The harness checks:
         – first line starts with “INCIDENT REPORT - ” and ends with “Z”
           (UTC ISO-8601)
         – the exact section titles
         – correct arrow notation of permissions
         – masked old secret (first 5 chars + “*****”)
         – new secret identical to .env.secure content
         – blocked IP present
         – no extra characters beyond final newline of the IP line
name: task_000000_eb7658f2
